name: Klaipeda County (Lithuania)

colors.symbolism: the standard outer field of Lithuanian county flags
the field of the county coat of arms

description: A blue field with a white inner panel bearing the coat of arms of Klaipeda County, a red maritime device referencing the historic seaport.
history: Like the flags of all ten Lithuanian counties, Klaipeda's flag follows a uniform template introduced after the country's redivision into apskritys (counties) in 1994: a blue outer border surrounding the county's heraldic field, here white, charged with the maritime arms of Klaipeda.

features.symbolism: blue outer border in the Lithuanian county pattern, with a white inner field bearing the county arms

features.role: coat of arms of Klaipeda County
features.symbolism: the county's heraldic device, including a red boat with a tower referencing the port city of Klaipeda and its maritime history
